attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: turn on the lamp to examine the bat
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and Their Types:
   - The scene contains a “lamp” (object type: object, not a receptacle) with a beige cylindrical shade and a metallic base, positioned on a wooden table. The lamp is currently off (isOn ?lamp is false).
   - A “baseball bat” (object type: object) is lying diagonally on the floor near the corner of the room, with a dark brown wooden handle and a metallic silver end cap. It is not held by any agent and is not inside any receptacle.
   - The wooden table (object type: receptacle, not explicitly openable) supports the lamp and a small white cup. The table is not openable and cannot be opened or closed.
   - A small wooden nightstand (object type: receptacle) is next to the bed, with a circular object (possibly a clock or decorative item) on top. It has drawers and is openable.
   - A book titled “Carpenter Vision” (object type: object) rests on the bed (receptacle).
   - A white cup (object type: object) is on the table next to the lamp.
   - A black trash bag (object type: object) is on the floor near the table leg.
   - A red chair (object type: object) is partially visible under the table.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 lamp
   - 1 baseball bat
   - 1 wooden table (receptacle)
   - 1 nightstand (receptacle)
   - 1 book
   - 1 white cup
   - 1 trash bag
   - 1 red chair
   - 1 bed (receptacle, partially visible)

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The lamp is on the wooden table (inReceptacle lamp table).
   - The white cup is on the wooden table (inReceptacle cup table).
   - The baseball bat is on the floor, near the corner and adjacent to the table leg.
   - The book is on the bed (inReceptacle book bed).
   - The circular object is on the nightstand (inReceptacle circular_object nightstand).
   - The trash bag is on the floor, not in any receptacle.
   - The red chair is under the table, partially visible.

4. Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:
   - The lamp is currently off (isOn lamp = false). It can be toggled on.
   - The baseball bat is not held by any agent and is not in any receptacle.
   - No agent is currently at any object’s location (atLocation agent ?object is false for all objects).
   - The lamp has not been toggled yet (isToggled lamp = false).

5. Visual Properties:
   - The lamp has a beige fabric shade and a gray metallic base. The bulb is visible and unlit.
   - The baseball bat is dark brown with a shiny silver end cap, lying on a brown carpeted floor.
   - The table is light brown wood with visible grain. The nightstand matches in color and material.
   - The book has a white cover with brown text and an image of a carpenter.
   - The white cup is cylindrical and plain.
   - The trash bag is black and crumpled.
   - The red chair has a maroon cushion and light wooden legs.

The robot must first navigate to the lamp’s location (on the table) to toggle it on, which will illuminate the scene and allow examination of the bat on the floor. The bat is not currently in any receptacle and is not being held. No other domain-relevant actions (e.g., cleaning, heating, slicing) are required for this task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem turn_on_lamp_to_examine_bat)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        lamp - object
        baseball_bat - object
        table - receptacle
        nightstand - receptacle
        book - object
        cup - object
        trash_bag - object
        red_chair - object
        bed - receptacle
        circular_object - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Objects are placed in their initial locations
        (inReceptacle lamp table)
        (inReceptacle cup table)
        (inReceptacle book bed)
        (inReceptacle circular_object nightstand)
        ; Lamp is initially off
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (isToggled lamp))
        ; Robot is not at any location initially
        (forall (?o - object)
            (not (atLocation robot ?o))
        )
        ; The baseball bat is on the floor (not in any receptacle)
        ; The trash bag is on the floor (not in any receptacle)
        ; The red chair is under the table (not relevant for toggling)
        ; The nightstand is openable (as per description)
        (openable nightstand)
        ; The table is not openable (as per description)
        ; No object is held by the robot
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (isOn lamp)
            (isToggled lamp)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem turn_on_lamp_to_examine_bat)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        lamp - object
        baseball_bat - object
        table - receptacle
        nightstand - receptacle
        book - object
        cup - object
        trash_bag - object
        red_chair - object
        bed - receptacle
        circular_object - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Objects are placed in their initial locations
        (inReceptacle lamp table)
        (inReceptacle cup table)
        (inReceptacle book bed)
        (inReceptacle circular_object nightstand)
        ; Lamp is initially off
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (isToggled lamp))
        ; Robot is not at any location initially
        (forall (?o - object)
            (not (atLocation robot ?o))
        )
        ; The baseball bat is on the floor (not in any receptacle)
        ; The trash bag is on the floor (not in any receptacle)
        ; The red chair is under the table (not relevant for toggling)
        ; The nightstand is openable (as per description)
        (openable nightstand)
        ; The table is not openable (as per description)
        ; No object is held by the robot
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (isOn lamp)
            (isToggled lamp)
        )
    )
)

Scene Description:

The scene depicts a room with several objects relevant to the robotic planning task. The primary objects of interest are a lamp and a baseball bat, as specified by the instruction to "turn on the lamp to examine the bat."

1. Objects and their types:
   - Lamp: A table lamp with a beige cylindrical shade and a metallic base. It is placed on a wooden table. The lamp is an object (not a receptacle) and is capable of being toggled (isOn predicate applies).
   - Baseball Bat: A brown wooden bat with a silver metallic end cap. It is lying diagonally on the floor next to the wooden table. The bat is an object (not a receptacle) and is not currently held or in any receptacle.
   - Wooden Table: A rectangular table with a light wood grain finish. It serves as a surface for the lamp and other small items. It is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, so it is treated as an object.
   - Nightstand: A small wooden nightstand with drawers, located next to a bed. It is not a receptacle in the domain, so it is treated as an object.
   - Bed: Partially visible with white bedding and a book titled “Carpenter Vision” on it. The bed is not a receptacle in the domain.
   - Book: A book titled “Carpenter Vision” lying on the bed. It is an object.
   - Small Objects: A pair of glasses and a small cup are on the wooden table near the lamp. These are objects.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 lamp
   - 1 baseball bat
   - 1 wooden table
   - 1 nightstand
   - 1 bed
   - 1 book
   - 2 small objects (glasses and cup)

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The lamp is on the wooden table.
   - The baseball bat is on the floor, adjacent to the wooden table and near a wall corner.
   - The nightstand is next to the bed.
   - The book is on the bed.
   - The glasses and cup are on the wooden table, near the lamp.

4. Current state of objects relevant to the planning task:
   - The lamp is currently off (isOn is false). It can be toggled to turn on.
   - The baseball bat is on the floor and is not being held or inside any receptacle.
   - The robot is not at any object’s location initially (atLocation robot ?o is false for all objects).

5. Colors and visual properties:
   - The lamp has a beige shade and a silver base.
   - The baseball bat is brown wood with a silver end cap.
   - The wooden table has a light wood grain finish.
   - The nightstand is a darker wood tone.
   - The bed has white bedding.
   - The book has a beige cover with visible text.
   - The glasses have orange and blue frames.
   - The cup is white.

The robot must first navigate to the lamp to toggle it on, which will illuminate the scene and allow for examination of the bat. The bat is not in a receptacle and is not being held, so no receptacle interaction is required for it. The lamp is the only object that needs to be toggled for this task.

```pddl
(define (problem turn_on_lamp_to_examine_bat)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        lamp - object
        baseball_bat - object
        wooden_table - object
        nightstand - object

        book - object
        glasses - object
        cup - object
    )

        (not (atLocation robot nightstand))

        (inReceptacle book bed)

    )
    (:goal (and (isOn lamp) (atLocation robot baseball_bat)))
)
```

            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

```pddl
(define (problem turn_on_lamp_to_examine_bat)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        lamp - object
        baseball_bat - object
        wooden_table - object
        nightstand - object
        bed - object
        book - object
        glasses - object
        cup - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot wooden_table) ; Assuming robot starts near the table for practicality; if not specified, could be omitted or set to a default
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot baseball_bat))
        (not (atLocation robot nightstand))
        (not (atLocation robot bed))
        (not (atLocation robot book))
        (not (atLocation robot glasses))
        (not (atLocation robot cup))
        (inReceptacle lamp wooden_table)
        (inReceptacle glasses wooden_table)
        (inReceptacle cup wooden_table)
        (inReceptacle book bed)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and (isOn lamp) (atLocation robot baseball_bat)))
)
```

(define (problem turn_on_lamp_to_examine_bat)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        lamp - object
        baseball_bat - object
        wooden_table - object
        nightstand - object
        bed - object
        book - object
        glasses - object
        cup - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot wooden_table) ; Assuming robot starts near the table for practicality; if not specified, could be omitted or set to a default
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot baseball_bat))
        (not (atLocation robot nightstand))
        (not (atLocation robot bed))
        (not (atLocation robot book))
        (not (atLocation robot glasses))
        (not (atLocation robot cup))
        (inReceptacle lamp wooden_table)
        (inReceptacle glasses wooden_table)
        (inReceptacle cup wooden_table)
